result.name: STL Batch Validator Report
result.description: Scans a directory of ASCII STL files and produces a manufacturing validation report checking manifold closure, inverted normals (winding-order), and degenerate triangles. Outputs console report and validation_report.json.

result.name: STL Batch Validator Report v2
result.description: Scans a directory of ASCII STL files matching supplier_*.stl and produces a manufacturing validation report checking manifold closure, inverted normals (winding-order cross-product), and degenerate triangles. Outputs a console report and validation_report.json. ASCII-safe output (no Unicode symbols).

result.name: Improved STL Validator
result.description: Validates ASCII STL files for manufacturing suitability: checks triangle count, surface area, manifold closure (open edges), inverted face normals, and degenerate triangles. Fixes the hidden inverted-normal bug present in stl_validator_provided.py.

result.name: Improved STL Validator v2
result.description: Validates ASCII STL files for manufacturing suitability: checks triangle count, surface area, manifold closure (open edges), inverted face normals via winding-order cross-product verification, and degenerate triangles. Fixes the hidden inverted-normal bug present in stl_validator_provided.py.

result.name: Improved STL Validator v3
result.description: Validates ASCII STL files for manufacturing suitability: checks triangle count, surface area, manifold closure (open edges), inverted face normals via winding-order cross-product verification, and degenerate (zero-area) triangles. Fixes the hidden inverted-normal bug present in stl_validator_provided.py. All 4 test cases pass.

result.name: Improved STL Validator v4
result.description: Validates ASCII STL files for manufacturing suitability: checks triangle count, surface area, manifold closure (open edges), inverted face normals via winding-order cross-product verification, and degenerate (zero-area) triangles. Fixes the hidden inverted-normal bug present in stl_validator_provided.py.

args.content:
"""Create sample STL files to validate."""

# 1. A valid closed tetrahedron (should pass all checks)
valid_tetrahedron = """solid tetrahedron
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 -1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 -1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal -1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.577350 0.577350 0.577350
    outer loop
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
endsolid tetrahedron
"""

# 2. An STL with inverted normals (normals point inward)
inverted_normals = """solid inverted
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal -0.577350 -0.577350 -0.577350
    outer loop
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
endsolid inverted
"""

# 3. An open mesh (missing a face)
open_mesh = """solid open_mesh
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 -1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 -1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal -1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
endsolid open_mesh
"""

# 4. A cube (valid closed solid)
cube = """solid cube
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 -1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 -1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 0.0 1.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 -1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 -1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 0.0 1.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 1.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal -1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal -1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 0.0 1.0 1.0
      vertex 0.0 0.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 0.0
    endloop
  endfacet
  facet normal 1.0 0.0 0.0
    outer loop
      vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
      vertex 1.0 0.0 1.0
      vertex 1.0 1.0 1.0
    endloop
  endfacet
endsolid cube
"""

with open("valid_tetrahedron.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(valid_tetrahedron)

with open("inverted_normals.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(inverted_normals)

with open("open_mesh.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(open_mesh)

with open("valid_cube.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(cube)

print("Created 4 test STL files.")

args.content:
"""Debug the normal checking logic."""
import math

def _cross(a, b):
    return (
        a[1] * b[2] - a[2] * b[1],
        a[2] * b[0] - a[0] * b[2],
        a[0] * b[1] - a[1] * b[0],
    )

def _sub(a, b):
    return (a[0] - b[0], a[1] - b[1], a[2] - b[2])

def _dot(a, b):
    return a[0] * b[0] + a[1] * b[1] + a[2] * b[2]

def _magnitude(v):
    return math.sqrt(v[0] ** 2 + v[1] ** 2 + v[2] ** 2)

# Test with first triangle from valid_tetrahedron.stl
# facet normal 0.0 0.0 -1.0
# vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0
# vertex 1.0 0.0 0.0
# vertex 0.0 1.0 0.0

declared_normal = (0.0, 0.0, -1.0)
v0 = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
v1 = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0)
v2 = (0.0, 1.0, 0.0)

computed_cross = _cross(_sub(v1, v0), _sub(v2, v0))
print(f"Computed cross product: {computed_cross}")
mag = _magnitude(computed_cross)
print(f"Magnitude: {mag}")
computed_normal = tuple(c / mag for c in computed_cross)
print(f"Computed normal: {computed_normal}")
print(f"Declared normal: {declared_normal}")

dn_mag = _magnitude(declared_normal)
dn_normalized = tuple(c / dn_mag for c in declared_normal)
dot = _dot(computed_normal, dn_normalized)
print(f"Dot product: {dot}")
print(f"Inverted: {dot < 0}")

print()
print("Analysis: The computed normal for a CCW triangle in xy-plane (z=0)")
print("  v0->v1 = (1,0,0), v0->v2 = (0,1,0)")
print("  cross = (0,0,1) [points +Z]")
print("  But declared normal is (0,0,-1) [points -Z]")
print("  This IS an inverted normal - the face's outward normal doesn't match winding")
print()
print("Wait - maybe the tetrahedron normals I wrote are wrong.")
print("For a tetrahedron with vertices at origin and unit axes:")
print("  Bottom face (z=0 plane): z=0, normal should point DOWN (-Z) for outward")
print("  But with winding order v0,v1,v2 = (0,0,0),(1,0,0),(0,1,0)")
print("  Right-hand rule gives normal = (0,0,+1) pointing UP")
print("  So the declared normal (0,0,-1) IS inverted relative to winding order")
print()
print("The STL test files I created have inconsistent normals/winding.")
print("Let me verify what the correct winding order should be for outward normals.")

  But with winding order v0,v1,v2 = (0,0,0),(1,0,0),(0,1,0)
  Right-hand rule gives normal = (0,0,+1) pointing UP

The STL test files I created have inconsistent normals/winding.
Let me verify what the correct winding order should be for outward normals.

args.content:
"""Create STL files with correct winding order and normals.

In STL format, normals should follow the right-hand rule:
If you curl your right hand fingers in the direction of vertex winding (CCW when
viewed from outside), your thumb points in the outward normal direction.

So: normal = normalize(cross(v1-v0, v2-v0))
"""
import math

def cross(a, b):
    return (
        a[1]*b[2] - a[2]*b[1],
        a[2]*b[0] - a[0]*b[2],
        a[0]*b[1] - a[1]*b[0],
    )

def sub(a, b):
    return (a[0]-b[0], a[1]-b[1], a[2]-b[2])

def normalize(v):
    mag = math.sqrt(v[0]**2 + v[1]**2 + v[2]**2)
    return tuple(c/mag for c in v)

def compute_normal(v0, v1, v2):
    return normalize(cross(sub(v1, v0), sub(v2, v0)))

def fmt_v(v):
    return f"{v[0]:.6f} {v[1]:.6f} {v[2]:.6f}"

def make_triangle(v0, v1, v2):
    n = compute_normal(v0, v1, v2)
    return f"  facet normal {fmt_v(n)}\n    outer loop\n      vertex {fmt_v(v0)}\n      vertex {fmt_v(v1)}\n      vertex {fmt_v(v2)}\n    endloop\n  endfacet\n"

# ========== 1. Valid tetrahedron (outward normals, correct winding) ==========
# Vertices: A=(0,0,0), B=(1,0,0), C=(0,1,0), D=(0,0,1)
# For outward normals, wind CCW when viewed from outside

A = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
B = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0)
C = (0.0, 1.0, 0.0)
D = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0)

# Face ABC (bottom, z=0): outward normal should point -Z, wind CW from above = CCW from below
# From below (-Z side): A->C->B is CCW, gives normal (0,0,-1) ✓
# Face ABD (front, y=0): outward normal should point -Y
# From front: A->B->D is CCW gives (0,-1,0)? Let's compute
print("Tetrahedron face normals:")
print(f"  ABC (A,C,B): {compute_normal(A,C,B)}")  # should be (0,0,-1)
print(f"  ABD (A,B,D): {compute_normal(A,B,D)}")  # should point outward
print(f"  ACD (A,D,C): {compute_normal(A,D,C)}")  # should point outward  
print(f"  BCD (B,C,D): {compute_normal(B,C,D)}")  # should point outward (away from origin)
print()

# Check - BCD should have positive components (away from origin at A)
# Centroid of BCD: ((1+0+0)/3, (0+1+0)/3, (0+0+1)/3) = (1/3, 1/3, 1/3)
# Normal should point away from A=(0,0,0), so dot((1/3,1/3,1/3), normal) > 0
bcd_n = compute_normal(B,C,D)
print(f"BCD normal dot with centroid direction: {sum(a*b for a,b in zip(bcd_n, (1/3,1/3,1/3)))}")

# For ABC (bottom face z=0), centroid = (1/3,1/3,0), outward = away from D=(0,0,1)
# So normal should have negative z
abc_n_acb = compute_normal(A,C,B)
abc_n_abc = compute_normal(A,B,C)
print(f"ABC with winding A,C,B: {abc_n_acb}  (should be negative z)")
print(f"ABC with winding A,B,C: {abc_n_abc}  (wrong - positive z)")
print()

tetra = "solid tetrahedron\n"
tetra += make_triangle(A, C, B)  # bottom face, outward = -Z
tetra += make_triangle(A, B, D)  # front face (y=0), outward = -Y 
tetra += make_triangle(A, D, C)  # left face (x=0), outward = -X
tetra += make_triangle(B, C, D)  # slanted face, outward away from origin
tetra += "endsolid tetrahedron\n"

print("Tetrahedron triangles:")
for line in tetra.split('\n'):
    if 'facet normal' in line or 'vertex' in line:
        print(f"  {line.strip()}")

with open("valid_tetrahedron.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(tetra)

# ========== 2. Cube (valid closed solid) ==========
# Unit cube from (0,0,0) to (1,1,1)
# Each face: wind CCW from outside

p000 = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
p100 = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0)
p010 = (0.0, 1.0, 0.0)
p110 = (1.0, 1.0, 0.0)
p001 = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
p101 = (1.0, 0.0, 1.0)
p011 = (0.0, 1.0, 1.0)
p111 = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0)

cube = "solid cube\n"
# Bottom face (z=0), outward = -Z: wind CW from above = CCW from below
cube += make_triangle(p000, p010, p100)  # -Z
cube += make_triangle(p010, p110, p100)  # -Z
# Top face (z=1), outward = +Z: wind CCW from above
cube += make_triangle(p001, p101, p011)  # +Z
cube += make_triangle(p101, p111, p011)  # +Z
# Front face (y=0), outward = -Y
cube += make_triangle(p000, p100, p001)  # -Y
cube += make_triangle(p100, p101, p001)  # -Y
# Back face (y=1), outward = +Y
cube += make_triangle(p010, p011, p110)  # +Y
cube += make_triangle(p011, p111, p110)  # +Y
# Left face (x=0), outward = -X
cube += make_triangle(p000, p001, p010)  # -X
cube += make_triangle(p001, p011, p010)  # -X
# Right face (x=1), outward = +X
cube += make_triangle(p100, p110, p101)  # +X
cube += make_triangle(p110, p111, p101)  # +X
cube += "endsolid cube\n"

with open("valid_cube.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(cube)

# ========== 3. Inverted normals (wrong winding or declared normal flipped) ==========
# Same tetrahedron but with normals declared as their negatives
tetra_inverted = "solid inverted_tetrahedron\n"
for line in tetra.split('\n'):
    if line.strip().startswith('facet normal'):
        parts = line.split()
        # Flip the declared normal
        nx, ny, nz = -float(parts[2]), -float(parts[3]), -float(parts[4])
        tetra_inverted += f"  facet normal {nx:.6f} {ny:.6f} {nz:.6f}\n"
    elif line.strip().startswith('solid') or line.strip().startswith('endsolid'):
        pass  # skip - we already added header
    else:
        tetra_inverted += line + "\n"
tetra_inverted += "endsolid inverted_tetrahedron\n"

# Actually let's do it properly
tetra_inv_lines = []
for _, verts in [
    ((A, C, B)),
    ((A, B, D)),
    ((A, D, C)),
    ((B, C, D)),
]:
    v0, v1, v2 = verts
    correct_n = compute_normal(v0, v1, v2)
    # Flip the normal declaration
    inv_n = tuple(-c for c in correct_n)
    tetra_inv_lines.append(
        f"  facet normal {fmt_v(inv_n)}\n    outer loop\n      vertex {fmt_v(v0)}\n      vertex {fmt_v(v1)}\n      vertex {fmt_v(v2)}\n    endloop\n  endfacet\n"
    )

inverted_stl = "solid inverted_normals\n" + "".join(tetra_inv_lines) + "endsolid inverted_normals\n"
with open("inverted_normals.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(inverted_stl)

# ========== 4. Open mesh (missing a face from the tetrahedron) ==========
open_stl = "solid open_mesh\n"
open_stl += make_triangle(A, C, B)  # bottom
open_stl += make_triangle(A, B, D)  # front
open_stl += make_triangle(A, D, C)  # left
# Missing face BCD - mesh is open
open_stl += "endsolid open_mesh\n"

with open("open_mesh.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(open_stl)

print("\nCreated 4 corrected STL files:")
print("  valid_tetrahedron.stl - valid closed solid")
print("  valid_cube.stl - valid closed solid")
print("  inverted_normals.stl - declared normals flipped (winding mismatch)")
print("  open_mesh.stl - missing one face")

args.content:
"""Create STL files with correct winding order and normals."""
import math

def cross(a, b):
    return (
        a[1]*b[2] - a[2]*b[1],
        a[2]*b[0] - a[0]*b[2],
        a[0]*b[1] - a[1]*b[0],
    )

def sub(a, b):
    return (a[0]-b[0], a[1]-b[1], a[2]-b[2])

def normalize(v):
    mag = math.sqrt(v[0]**2 + v[1]**2 + v[2]**2)
    return tuple(c/mag for c in v)

def compute_normal(v0, v1, v2):
    return normalize(cross(sub(v1, v0), sub(v2, v0)))

def fmt_v(v):
    return f"{v[0]:.6f} {v[1]:.6f} {v[2]:.6f}"

def make_triangle(v0, v1, v2):
    n = compute_normal(v0, v1, v2)
    return (f"  facet normal {fmt_v(n)}\n"
            f"    outer loop\n"
            f"      vertex {fmt_v(v0)}\n"
            f"      vertex {fmt_v(v1)}\n"
            f"      vertex {fmt_v(v2)}\n"
            f"    endloop\n"
            f"  endfacet\n")

def make_triangle_with_normal(v0, v1, v2, n):
    return (f"  facet normal {fmt_v(n)}\n"
            f"    outer loop\n"
            f"      vertex {fmt_v(v0)}\n"
            f"      vertex {fmt_v(v1)}\n"
            f"      vertex {fmt_v(v2)}\n"
            f"    endloop\n"
            f"  endfacet\n")

# Tetrahedron vertices
A = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
B = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0)
C = (0.0, 1.0, 0.0)
D = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0)

# ========== 1. Valid tetrahedron ==========
tetra = "solid tetrahedron\n"
tetra += make_triangle(A, C, B)  # bottom face (z=0), outward = -Z
tetra += make_triangle(A, B, D)  # front face (y=0), outward = -Y
tetra += make_triangle(A, D, C)  # left face (x=0), outward = -X
tetra += make_triangle(B, C, D)  # slanted face, outward away from origin
tetra += "endsolid tetrahedron\n"

with open("valid_tetrahedron.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(tetra)
print("Created valid_tetrahedron.stl")

# ========== 2. Valid cube ==========
p000 = (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
p100 = (1.0, 0.0, 0.0)
p010 = (0.0, 1.0, 0.0)
p110 = (1.0, 1.0, 0.0)
p001 = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
p101 = (1.0, 0.0, 1.0)
p011 = (0.0, 1.0, 1.0)
p111 = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0)

cube = "solid cube\n"
# Bottom face (z=0), outward = -Z
cube += make_triangle(p000, p010, p100)
cube += make_triangle(p010, p110, p100)
# Top face (z=1), outward = +Z
cube += make_triangle(p001, p101, p011)
cube += make_triangle(p101, p111, p011)
# Front face (y=0), outward = -Y
cube += make_triangle(p000, p100, p001)
cube += make_triangle(p100, p101, p001)
# Back face (y=1), outward = +Y
cube += make_triangle(p010, p011, p110)
cube += make_triangle(p011, p111, p110)
# Left face (x=0), outward = -X
cube += make_triangle(p000, p001, p010)
cube += make_triangle(p001, p011, p010)
# Right face (x=1), outward = +X
cube += make_triangle(p100, p110, p101)
cube += make_triangle(p110, p111, p101)
cube += "endsolid cube\n"

with open("valid_cube.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(cube)
print("Created valid_cube.stl")

# ========== 3. Inverted normals (declared normals flipped) ==========
# Same tetrahedron geometry but all declared normals are negated
tetra_faces = [
    (A, C, B),
    (A, B, D),
    (A, D, C),
    (B, C, D),
]

inverted_stl = "solid inverted_normals\n"
for (v0, v1, v2) in tetra_faces:
    correct_n = compute_normal(v0, v1, v2)
    inv_n = (-correct_n[0], -correct_n[1], -correct_n[2])
    inverted_stl += make_triangle_with_normal(v0, v1, v2, inv_n)
inverted_stl += "endsolid inverted_normals\n"

with open("inverted_normals.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(inverted_stl)
print("Created inverted_normals.stl")

# ========== 4. Open mesh (missing one face) ==========
open_stl = "solid open_mesh\n"
open_stl += make_triangle(A, C, B)  # bottom
open_stl += make_triangle(A, B, D)  # front
open_stl += make_triangle(A, D, C)  # left
# Missing face (B, C, D) - mesh is open
open_stl += "endsolid open_mesh\n"

with open("open_mesh.stl", "w") as f:
    f.write(open_stl)
print("Created open_mesh.stl")

print("\nDone creating 4 test STL files.")

args.content:
"""Test the final improved validator against all 4 test STL files."""
import sys
sys.argv = ['test']
import importlib.util

spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("improved", "stl_validator_improved_final.py")
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mod)

test_cases = [
    ("valid_tetrahedron.stl",  True,  "Valid closed tetrahedron"),
    ("valid_cube.stl",         True,  "Valid closed cube"),
    ("inverted_normals.stl",   False, "Tetrahedron with all normals flipped"),
    ("open_mesh.stl",          False, "Open mesh (missing one face)"),
]

all_pass = True
print("Testing improved STL validator...\n")
for fname, expected_valid, description in test_cases:
    result = mod.parse_stl(fname)
    actual_valid = result['valid']
    status = "PASS" if actual_valid == expected_valid else "FAIL"
    if actual_valid != expected_valid:
        all_pass = False
    print(f"[{status}] {fname}")
    print(f"       Description: {description}")
    print(f"       Expected valid={expected_valid}, Got valid={actual_valid}")
    print(f"       Triangles: {result['triangle_count']}, Surface Area: {result['surface_area']}")
    if result['issues']:
        for issue in result['issues']:
            print(f"       Issue: {issue}")
    print()

print("=" * 50)
if all_pass:
    print("ALL TESTS PASSED")
else:
    print("SOME TESTS FAILED")
    sys.exit(1)
